title: Free Variables of a Formula
statement:
The set $\mathrm{FV}(\varphi)$ of \emph{free variables} of a formula $\varphi$
is defined recursively:
- If $\varphi = P(t_1,\dots,t_n)$ is atomic, then
 $\mathrm{FV}(\varphi) = \bigcup_{i=1}^n \mathrm{Var}(t_i)$,
 where $\mathrm{Var}(t_i)$ is the set of variables in term $t_i$.
- $\mathrm{FV}(\neg \varphi) = \mathrm{FV}(\varphi)$.
- $\mathrm{FV}(\varphi \circ \psi) = \mathrm{FV}(\varphi) \cup \mathrm{FV}(\psi)$
 for any binary connective $\circ$.
- $\mathrm{FV}(\forall x\,\varphi) = \mathrm{FV}(\varphi) \setminus \{x\}$.
- $\mathrm{FV}(\exists x\,\varphi) = \mathrm{FV}(\varphi) \setminus \{x\}$.
